text:
Australian hockey sensation Matt Dawson has made the extraordinary decision to amputate part of his finger to secure his spot at the upcomingParis Olympic Games.

The 30-year-old suffered a severe injury to his right hand during training and faced with a two-month recovery period post-surgery, he opted for amputation from the knuckle upwards to ensure his participation in Paris.

Despite his wife's plea against any "rash" actions following the mishap, Dawson, concerned this might be his last shot at Olympic glory, chose to undergo amputation on the day of the injury.

"I'm definitely closer to the end of my career than the start and, who knows, this could be my last [Olympics], and if I felt that I could still perform at my best then that's what I was going to do," Dawson revealed on theParlez Vous Hockeypodcast. "If taking the top of my finger was the price I had to pay, that's what I would do."

Previously, in 2018, Dawson nearly lost an eye after a pre-Commonwealth Games incident involving a hockey stick, yet he persevered and contributed to Australia's gold medal victory.

Pick up your ultimate guide to the Olympics! Going for Gold provides the day-by-day rundown of what's on and when. Clickhereto buy.



Aran Zalewski, captain of the Australian team, admitted that Dawson's drastic choice stunned his teammates, but acknowledged that for Dawson, who is deeply invested in his Olympic journey, the decision was straightforward.

"We didn't really know what to think, and then we heard that he went to the hospital and chopped his finger off, which was pretty interesting because I know people would give an arm and a leg and even a little bit of finger to be here sometimes," he commented.

"When you've spent a lifetime of choice and sacrifice to come and compete at the highest level, I think for him it was an easy decision."

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

Their coach, Colin Batch, confirmed just days before that Dawson had resumed full training, sharing with Australian TV: "Full marks to Matt. Obviously he's really committed to playing in Paris. I'm not sure I would have done it, but he's done it, so great."

Australia, who suffered defeat to Belgium via penalties in the finals three years back, are set to commence their campaign on Saturday lunchtime against Argentina. They share the same group as Ireland and Belgium.

Team GB will face Spain, South Africa and the Netherlands in Pool A.

The Hockey competitions are scheduled to unfold at the Stade Olympique Yves-du-Manoir situated on the North Western precinct of Paris.

title: Olympics star amputates finger just to compete after horror injury | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Alan Smith
description: One Olympian is set to compete in Paris this summer but only after choosing to amputate his finger.
